_source: Adapted from DeflockSC (github.com/TimSimpsonJr/deflocksc-website), MIT. Camera counts via EFF Atlas of Surveillance / Flock transparency portals. Verify before filing.

agencies.name: Greenville Police Department
agencies.notes: Has refused to disclose camera locations publicly.

agencies.name: City of Greenville — City Clerk
agencies.notes: For budget approvals and council minutes on surveillance technology.

agencies.name: Greenville County — Clerk to Council
agencies.notes: For county council minutes and surveillance-spending records.

agencies.name: Greer Police Department
agencies.notes: 4 Flock cameras confirmed via EFF Atlas of Surveillance.

agencies.name: Mauldin Police Department
agencies.notes: 6 Flock cameras confirmed via EFF Atlas of Surveillance.

agencies.name: Travelers Rest Police Department
agencies.notes: Listed on Flock transparency portal; count not documented.

agencies.name: Spartanburg Police Department
agencies.notes: 11 fixed + 4 mobile Flock cameras; actively promotes its program.

agencies.name: Spartanburg County Sheriff's Office
agencies.notes: Former Sheriff Chuck Wright pled guilty to federal charges; he had unsupervised access to these cameras.

agencies.name: Spartanburg County — Clerk to Council
agencies.notes: For county council minutes and surveillance-spending records.

agencies.name: City of Spartanburg — City Clerk
agencies.notes: For budget approvals and minutes on Spartanburg PD's Flock contract.

agencies.name: Anderson Police Department
agencies.notes: 22 Flock cameras confirmed via EFF Atlas of Surveillance.

agencies.name: Anderson County Sheriff's Office
agencies.notes: 10 cameras confirmed; no local oversight ordinance.

agencies.name: Anderson County — Clerk to Council
agencies.notes: For county council minutes and surveillance-spending records.
